items.name: Kage
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Kage – Shadow any website to a single binary for offline viewing
items.summary: Shadow websites into a single offline binary.
items.why_included: New Show HN tool with a concrete archiving use case and strong comment interest.
items.evidence: Commenters said Kage looks promising for offline company wikis and compared it with SingleFile/HTTrack-style archiving.

items.name: SingleFile
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Kage – Shadow any website to a single binary for offline viewing
items.summary: Browser extension and CLI for saving faithful one-file copies of pages.
items.why_included: Repeatedly praised as the clean-save baseline in the Kage thread.
items.evidence: A commenter called SingleFile their clean-save default and said Kage would be compelling if it matched SingleFile quality.

items.name: SQL→ER diagram tool
items.source_story_title: Free SQL→ER diagram tool, runs in the browser, nothing uploaded
items.summary: Browser ER-diagrammer for SQL schemas with no uploads.
items.why_included: Practical browser-only workflow tool with direct praise from commenters.
items.evidence: Commenters called it useful and great, while suggesting explain.dalibo, wwwsqldesigner, and datagram.studio as comparisons.

items.name: Ollama Dash
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Dashboard for ranking local and hosted LLMs.
items.why_included: Concrete AI ops utility described by its builder in the Ask HN thread.
items.evidence: The poster said it combines ArtificialAnalysis, the Ollama registry, and OpenRouter data for model ranking.

items.name: Laravel OpenAPI
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: API documentation generator for Laravel apps.
items.why_included: Clear developer utility with framework-specific ergonomics.
items.evidence: The poster said it understands Laravel conventions and the ecosystem, rather than treating Laravel like a generic PHP app.

items.name: Whistle Enterprise
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Voice-transcription product built around fine-tuned LLMs.
items.why_included: Specific product launch with a narrow, understandable use case.
items.evidence: The builder said they fell down the voice-transcription rabbit hole and built Whistle Enterprise after pairing it with fine-tuned LLM work.

items.name: Astrolabe
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Nextcloud semantic layer and MCP backend.
items.why_included: Local-first + MCP angle makes it a concrete workflow tool, not just another SaaS.
items.evidence: The poster described Astrolabe as a Nextcloud app plus backend and said they use it as an MCP server across apps.

items.name: Inference Club
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Distributed inference network for consumer hardware.
items.why_included: Distinctive infrastructure product with a concrete deployment model.
items.evidence: The poster said you sign in with GitHub, run an agent on your home network, and register local inference resources.

items.name: Hiring Method
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Hiring platform that avoids black-box AI screening.
items.why_included: Clear product stance in a crowded HR-tech category.
items.evidence: The poster said v1 is live after two pivots and contrasted it with HR tech rushing to use black-box AI.

items.name: Fungible
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: What are you working on? (June 2026)
items.summary: Local-first personal finance app with TUI and Electron GUI.
items.why_included: Concrete, self-contained personal software with both terminal and GUI modes.
items.evidence: The poster said Fungible is free, open source, local-first, and available as both a TUI and an Electron app.
